summary: Postinstall pipes unpinned remote shell script to sh and sudo-installs an unverified binary to /usr/local/bin
details: On `npm install`, the postinstall hook in scripts/install.js executes `curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/neutron420/StackAudit/main/scripts/install.sh | sh` (and an equivalent `iwr... | iex` on Windows). The URL points at the mutable `main` branch with no commit SHA, no hash, and no signature verification. The fetched install.sh then queries GitHub's `releases/latest` endpoint (also mutable), downloads the first matching tarball over `curl -L | tar -xz`, and runs `sudo mv stack /usr/local/bin/` — installing an unverified binary to a system path under root. Any future change to the `main` branch or the `latest` release — whether by the maintainer or by anyone who compromises the GitHub account — results in arbitrary root-level code execution on every machine that installs this package. There is no integrity check at any stage of the chain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
